mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean direction in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate decline below 4.0% signals extreme labor market tightness that forces corporate wage increases, boosting near-term earnings expectations and driving positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >60% of instances when UNRATE falls below 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and housing starts indicates a broad demand contraction that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns exceed zero in >50% of periods when both GDPC1 and HOUST decline, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hikes, producing positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of tightening periods when INDPRO rises, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by >2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, the cost of capital for leveraged buyouts and corporate refinancing spikes, triggering immediate negative market repricing over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in a piecewise regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total employment combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates robust job creation without wage inflation pressure, supporting corporate margins and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >50% of periods when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE is flat, or the joint signal shows negative information coefficient over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real personal consumption expenditures directly reduce corporate sales expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of periods when RSAFS declines, or the signal predictive power drops below a Rank IC of 0.01 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production following a supply chain disruption signals capacity restoration that alleviates input cost pressures, generating positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following an INDPRO rebound, or the mechanism fails to produce statistically significant alpha (t-stat < 1.96) in event studies.
